---
title: "Community Discussion Topic Classifier"
format: 
    pdf:
        fig-width: 7
        fig-height: 4
        fig-asp: 0.7
---


#### Install Required Libraries

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
import subprocess
import sys
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
from datasets import load_dataset
import nltk
nltk.download('punkt')
nltk.download('stopwords')
nltk.download('wordnet')


```

#### Loading the Dataset

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import requests
import json
from io import StringIO
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

print("Loading TweetTopic dataset directly from source...")

# The dataset is stored as JSON files on Hugging Face
base_url = "https://huggingface.co/datasets/cardiffnlp/tweet_topic_single/resolve/main/dataset"

# Define the splits we want to load [citation:2]
splits = {
    "train": f"{base_url}/split_temporal/train_2020.single.json",
    "test": f"{base_url}/split_temporal/test_2021.single.json",
    "validation": f"{base_url}/split_temporal/validation_2020.single.json"
}

# Load each split
data_frames = {}

for split_name, url in splits.items():
    print(f"Loading {split_name} split...")
    
    try:
        # Download the JSON file
        response = requests.get(url)
        response.raise_for_status()
        
        # Parse JSON lines (each line is a separate JSON object)
        json_lines = response.text.strip().split('\n')
        split_data = []
        
        for line in json_lines:
            if line.strip():  # Skip empty lines
                try:
                    tweet = json.loads(line)
                    split_data.append(tweet)
                except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
                    print(f"  Warning: Could not parse line: {e}")
                    continue
        
        # Convert to DataFrame
        df = pd.DataFrame(split_data)
        data_frames[split_name] = df
        print(f"  ✓ Loaded {len(df)} tweets")
        
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"  ✗ Error loading {split_name}: {e}")
        data_frames[split_name] = pd.DataFrame()  # Empty DataFrame as fallback

# Access each split
train_df = data_frames.get("train", pd.DataFrame())
test_df = data_frames.get("test", pd.DataFrame())
val_df = data_frames.get("validation", pd.DataFrame())

print(f"\n✅ Dataset loaded successfully!")
print(f"Training set: {len(train_df)} samples")
print(f"Test set: {len(test_df)} samples")
print(f"Validation set: {len(val_df)} samples")

# Show the data structure
if len(train_df) > 0:
    print("\nFirst few rows:")
    print(train_df[['text', 'label_name', 'date']].head())
    
    print("\nTopic distribution:")
    print(train_df['label_name'].value_counts())
    
    print(f"\nTopics: {sorted(train_df['label_name'].unique())}")
```

#### Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

```{python}

from wordcloud import WordCloud
import re
from collections import Counter

# Basic statistics
print("Dataset Overview:")
print(f"Total samples: {len(train_df) + len(test_df) + len(val_df)}")
print(f"Topics: {train_df['label_name'].unique()}")

# Topic distribution
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))

# Training set distribution
topic_counts_train = train_df['label_name'].value_counts()
sns.barplot(x=topic_counts_train.values, y=topic_counts_train.index, ax=axes[0], palette='viridis')
axes[0].set_title('Training Set Topic Distribution')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Number of Samples')

# Test set distribution  
topic_counts_test = test_df['label_name'].value_counts()
sns.barplot(x=topic_counts_test.values, y=topic_counts_test.index, ax=axes[1], palette='plasma')
axes[1].set_title('Test Set Topic Distribution')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Number of Samples')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Text length analysis
train_df['text_length'] = train_df['text'].str.len()
test_df['text_length'] = test_df['text'].str.len()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 4))

axes[0].hist(train_df['text_length'], bins=30, alpha=0.7, color='blue', edgecolor='black')
axes[0].set_title('Training Set: Text Length Distribution')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Text Length (characters)')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Frequency')

axes[1].hist(test_df['text_length'], bins=30, alpha=0.7, color='green', edgecolor='black')
axes[1].set_title('Test Set: Text Length Distribution')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Text Length (characters)')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Frequency')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"Average text length: {train_df['text_length'].mean():.0f} characters")
print(f"Median text length: {train_df['text_length'].median():.0f} characters")
```

#### Text Preprocessing
```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true

import nltk
import re
import pandas as pd
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Function to safely download NLTK data
def download_nltk_resources():
    """Download required NLTK resources with error handling"""
    resources = ['punkt', 'stopwords', 'wordnet', 'averaged_perceptron_tagger']
    
    for resource in resources:
        try:
            print(f"Downloading {resource}...")
            nltk.download(resource, quiet=True)
            print(f"  ✓ {resource} downloaded")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"  ✗ Error downloading {resource}: {e}")
    
    # Test if punkt works
    try:
        nltk.word_tokenize("Test sentence.")
        print("✓ NLTK resources are working!")
    except LookupError as e:
        print(f"✗ Still having issues: {e}")
        # Alternative: download to specific path
        nltk.download('punkt', download_dir='/tmp/nltk_data')
        nltk.data.path.append('/tmp/nltk_data')

# Download resources
download_nltk_resources()

# Now import the rest
from nltk.corpus import stopwords
from nltk.stem import WordNetLemmatizer

class TextPreprocessor:
    def __init__(self):
        # Initialize with fallbacks
        try:
            self.stop_words = set(stopwords.words('english'))
        except LookupError:
            # If still not available, use a basic set
            nltk.download('stopwords', quiet=True)
            self.stop_words = set(stopwords.words('english'))
        
        try:
            self.lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()
        except LookupError:
            nltk.download('wordnet', quiet=True)
            self.lemmatizer = WordNetLemmatizer()
        
        # Add custom stopwords
        custom_stops = {'rt', 'via', 'amp', 'get', 'got', 'got', 'dont', 'didnt'}
        self.stop_words.update(custom_stops)
        
    def clean_tweet(self, text):
        """Clean a single tweet"""
        if not isinstance(text, str):
            return ""
        
        # Convert to lowercase
        text = text.lower()
        
        # Remove URLs
        text = re.sub(r'http\S+|www\S+|https\S+', '', text, flags=re.MULTILINE)
        
        # Remove user mentions (@username)
        text = re.sub(r'@\w+', '', text)
        
        # Handle hashtags - keep the text after #
        text = re.sub(r'#(\w+)', r'\1', text)
        
        # Remove special characters and digits (but keep letters)
        text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s]', ' ', text)
        text = re.sub(r'\d+', '', text)
        
        # Remove extra whitespace
        text = ' '.join(text.split())
        
        return text
    
    def preprocess(self, text):
        """Full preprocessing pipeline with error handling"""
        try:
            # Clean
            text = self.clean_tweet(text)
            
            if not text:  # If text is empty after cleaning
                return ""
            
            # Tokenize with fallback
            try:
                tokens = nltk.word_tokenize(text)
            except LookupError:
                # Simple fallback tokenization
                tokens = text.split()
            
            # Remove stopwords and lemmatize
            processed_tokens = []
            for token in tokens:
                if token not in self.stop_words and len(token) > 2:
                    try:
                        token = self.lemmatizer.lemmatize(token)
                    except:
                        pass  # Keep original if lemmatization fails
                    processed_tokens.append(token)
            
            return ' '.join(processed_tokens)
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Warning: Error preprocessing text: {e}")
            return text  # Return original if preprocessing fails

# Initialize preprocessor
preprocessor = TextPreprocessor()

# Test on a few samples first
print("Testing preprocessor on samples:")
test_samples = train_df['text'].head(3).tolist()
for original in test_samples:
    cleaned = preprocessor.preprocess(original)
    print(f"\nOriginal: {original[:100]}...")
    print(f"Cleaned:  {cleaned[:100]}...")

# Apply preprocessing to datasets
print("\nPreprocessing training data...")
train_df['clean_text'] = train_df['text'].apply(preprocessor.preprocess)

print("Preprocessing test data...")
test_df['clean_text'] = test_df['text'].apply(preprocessor.preprocess)

print("Preprocessing validation data...")
val_df['clean_text'] = val_df['text'].apply(preprocessor.preprocess)

# Check results
print(f"\n✅ Preprocessing complete!")
print(f"Training samples processed: {len(train_df[train_df['clean_text'] != ''])} / {len(train_df)}")
print(f"Test samples processed: {len(test_df[test_df['clean_text'] != ''])} / {len(test_df)}")

# Show sample
print("\nSample results:")
for i in range(3):
    print(f"\nOriginal ({i+1}): {train_df['text'].iloc[i][:100]}...")
    print(f"Cleaned ({i+1}):  {train_df['clean_text'].iloc[i][:100]}...")

# Add text length after preprocessing
train_df['clean_length'] = train_df['clean_text'].str.len()
print(f"\nAverage cleaned text length: {train_df['clean_length'].mean():.0f} characters")
```

#### Feature Extraction and Model Building

```{python}

from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.svm import LinearSVC
from sklearn.metrics import classification_report, confusion_matrix, accuracy_score
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Prepare features
print("Creating TF-IDF features...")
tfidf = TfidfVectorizer(max_features=5000, 
                        ngram_range=(1, 2),
                        min_df=5,
                        max_df=0.7)

X_train = tfidf.fit_transform(train_df['clean_text'])
X_test = tfidf.transform(test_df['clean_text'])

# Encode labels
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
label_encoder = LabelEncoder()
y_train = label_encoder.fit_transform(train_df['label_name'])
y_test = label_encoder.transform(test_df['label_name'])

print(f"Feature matrix shape: {X_train.shape}")
print(f"Classes: {label_encoder.classes_}")

# Define models to try
models = {
    'Naive Bayes': MultinomialNB(),
    'Logistic Regression': LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=42),
    'Linear SVM': LinearSVC(random_state=42, max_iter=2000),
    'Random Forest': RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=42)
}

# Train and evaluate each model
results = {}
for name, model in models.items():
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"Training {name}...")
    
    # Train
    model.fit(X_train, y_train)
    
    # Predict
    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
    
    # Calculate accuracy
    accuracy = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)
    results[name] = accuracy
    
    print(f"Accuracy: {accuracy:.4f}")
    print(f"\nClassification Report:")
    print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred, target_names=label_encoder.classes_))
    
    # Confusion Matrix
    cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
    sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt='d', cmap='Blues', 
                xticklabels=label_encoder.classes_,
                yticklabels=label_encoder.classes_)
    plt.title(f'Confusion Matrix - {name}')
    plt.ylabel('True Label')
    plt.xlabel('Predicted Label')
    plt.xticks(rotation=45)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# Compare models
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
model_names = list(results.keys())
accuracies = list(results.values())

bars = plt.bar(model_names, accuracies, color=['blue', 'green', 'orange', 'red'])
plt.ylabel('Accuracy')
plt.title('Model Comparison')
plt.ylim([0, 1])

# Add value labels on bars
for bar, acc in zip(bars, accuracies):
    plt.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2, bar.get_height() + 0.01,
             f'{acc:.3f}', ha='center', va='bottom')

plt.show()

print(f"\nBest Model: {max(results, key=results.get)} with accuracy {max(results.values()):.4f}")
```

#### Topic Discovery (Unsupervised) with BERTopic
```{python}

from bertopic import BERTopic
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer

# Initialize BERTopic
print("Initializing BERTopic for topic discovery...")
sentence_model = SentenceTransformer("all-MiniLM-L6-v2")

# Customize vectorizer to remove common words
vectorizer = CountVectorizer(stop_words="english", min_df=5, max_df=0.7)

# Create BERTopic model
topic_model = BERTopic(
    embedding_model=sentence_model,
    vectorizer_model=vectorizer,
    verbose=True,
    min_topic_size=10,
    nr_topics='auto'  # Automatically reduce topics
)

# Fit on training data
print("Discovering topics in training data...")
topics, probs = topic_model.fit_transform(train_df['clean_text'])

# Get topic info
topic_info = topic_model.get_topic_info()
print(f"\nDiscovered {len(topic_info[topic_info.Topic != -1])} topics (excluding outliers)")

# Show top topics
print("\nTop 5 discovered topics:")
for i in range(min(5, len(topic_info))):
    if topic_info.iloc[i]['Topic'] != -1:
        print(f"\nTopic {topic_info.iloc[i]['Topic']}:")
        print(f"Size: {topic_info.iloc[i]['Count']} documents")
        print(f"Name: {topic_info.iloc[i]['Name']}")
        print(f"Representation: {topic_info.iloc[i]['Representation'][:5]}")

# Visualize topics
fig = topic_model.visualize_topics()
fig.show()

# Visualize topic hierarchy
fig = topic_model.visualize_hierarchy()
fig.show()

# Compare discovered topics with actual labels
train_df['discovered_topic'] = topics

# Create a confusion matrix between actual and discovered topics
topic_mapping = pd.crosstab(train_df['label_name'], train_df['discovered_topic'])
print("\nMapping between actual labels and discovered topics:")
print(topic_mapping)
```

#### Building the Channel Recommender
```{python}
class ChannelRecommender:
    def __init__(self, classifier, topic_model, label_encoder, tfidf_vectorizer):
        self.classifier = classifier
        self.topic_model = topic_model
        self.label_encoder = label_encoder
        self.tfidf = tfidf_vectorizer
        self.channel_descriptions = {
            'arts_&_culture': '🎨 Arts & Culture - Discussing art, museums, exhibitions, and cultural events',
            'business_&_entrepreneurs': '💼 Business & Entrepreneurs - Startups, business news, and entrepreneurship',
            'daily_life': '🌅 Daily Life - Everyday experiences, personal stories, and life updates',
            'pop_culture': '🎬 Pop Culture - Movies, music, celebrities, and entertainment news',
            'science_&_technology': '🔬 Science & Tech - Latest in tech, scientific discoveries, and innovation',
            'sports_&_gaming': '🏆 Sports & Gaming - Sports discussions, video games, and esports'
        }
        
    def recommend(self, text, top_k=3):
        """Recommend channels for a given text"""
        
        # Preprocess the text
        clean_text = preprocessor.preprocess(text)
        
        # Method 1: Supervised classification
        X_new = self.tfidf.transform([clean_text])
        topic_proba = self.classifier.predict_proba(X_new)[0]
        
        # Get top predicted channels
        top_indices = np.argsort(topic_proba)[-top_k:][::-1]
        supervised_channels = [(self.label_encoder.inverse_transform([i])[0], 
                               topic_proba[i]) for i in top_indices]
        
        # Method 2: Topic modeling similarity
        topics, probs = self.topic_model.transform([clean_text])
        similar_topics = self.topic_model.find_topics(clean_text, top_n=top_k)
        
        # Map discovered topics to actual channels (simplified)
        # In practice, you'd want a more sophisticated mapping
        channel_mapping = {
            0: 'sports_&_gaming',
            1: 'science_&_technology',
            2: 'pop_culture',
            3: 'business_&_entrepreneurs',
            4: 'daily_life',
            5: 'arts_&_culture'
        }
        
        topic_based_channels = []
        if similar_topics[0]:
            for topic_id, similarity in zip(similar_topics[0][:top_k], similar_topics[1][:top_k]):
                if topic_id in channel_mapping:
                    topic_based_channels.append((channel_mapping[topic_id], similarity))
        
        return {
            'supervised_recommendations': supervised_channels,
            'topic_model_recommendations': topic_based_channels,
            'text': text,
            'clean_text': clean_text
        }
    
    def display_recommendations(self, recommendations):
        """Pretty print recommendations"""
        print(f"📝 Input Text: {recommendations['text'][:150]}...\n")
        
        print("🎯 Supervised Learning Recommendations:")
        for channel, confidence in recommendations['supervised_recommendations']:
            print(f"  {self.channel_descriptions[channel]}")
            print(f"  Confidence: {confidence:.2%}\n")
        
        if recommendations['topic_model_recommendations']:
            print("🔄 Topic Modeling Recommendations:")
            for channel, similarity in recommendations['topic_model_recommendations']:
                print(f"  {self.channel_descriptions[channel]}")
                print(f"  Similarity: {similarity:.2%}\n")

# Train the best classifier (Logistic Regression usually performs well)
best_classifier = LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=42)
best_classifier.fit(X_train, y_train)

# Create recommender
recommender = ChannelRecommender(
    classifier=best_classifier,
    topic_model=topic_model,
    label_encoder=label_encoder,
    tfidf_vectorizer=tfidf
)

# Test on some examples
test_texts = [
    "Just watched the new Marvel movie, it was amazing! The acting was superb.",
    "My startup just got funded by a VC firm! Time to scale our AI product.",
    "The Lakers game last night was incredible, LeBron scored 40 points!",
    "Visited the Met museum today, the new exhibition is breathtaking.",
    "Working from home today, just making coffee and relaxing."
]

print("="*60)
print("CHANNEL RECOMMENDER TEST")
print("="*60)

for i, text in enumerate(test_texts, 1):
    print(f"\n--- Example {i} ---")
    recommendations = recommender.recommend(text)
    recommender.display_recommendations(recommendations)
```

####    Create a Simple Demo App
```{python}

#| echo: true
#| eval: true

import ipywidgets as widgets
from IPython.display import display, clear_output

# Create widgets
text_input = widgets.Textarea(
    value='',
    placeholder='Type your message here...',
    description='Message:',
    disabled=False,
    layout=widgets.Layout(width='100%', height='100px')
)

recommend_button = widgets.Button(
    description='Get Recommendations',
    button_style='primary',
    layout=widgets.Layout(width='200px')
)

output = widgets.Output()

def on_button_clicked(b):
    with output:
        clear_output()
        if text_input.value:
            recommendations = recommender.recommend(text_input.value)
            recommender.display_recommendations(recommendations)
        else:
            print("Please enter a message!")

recommend_button.on_click(on_button_clicked)

# Display the demo
print("🎯 Community Discussion Channel Recommender")
print("="*50)
display(text_input)
display(recommend_button)
display(output)

# Add some example buttons
print("\nTry these examples:")
examples = [
    "Anyone watching the Oscars tonight?",
    "Bitcoin just hit a new all-time high!",
    "The new iPhone camera is incredible",
    "Working on my side project this weekend"
]

for i, example in enumerate(examples, 1):
    button = widgets.Button(description=f"Example {i}", layout=widgets.Layout(width='120px'))
    def create_handler(text):
        def handler(b):
            text_input.value = text
            on_button_clicked(None)
        return handler
    button.on_click(create_handler(example))
    display(button)
```
